When you use the Aberrant Fortitude benefit of your Aberrant Dragonmark feat, you can roll {@dice 1d6} instead of {@dice 1d4}. You also now regain your use of Aberrant Fortitude whenever you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Greater Aberrant Mark.entries.1.name: Improved Fortitude
When you cast a cantrip, you can roll one or two of your unexpended {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB}. You gain a number of {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the number rolled plus your Constitution modifier, and one creature of your choice within 30 feet of you (not including you) takes Force damage equal to the number rolled. Those dice are then expended.
You can use this benefit a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Greater Aberrant Mark.entries.2.name: Mark of Inspiration
Greater Aberrant Mark.name: Greater Aberrant Mark
Greater Mark of Detection.entries.1.entr: When you use the Deductive Intuition benefit of your {@feat Mark of Detection|EFA} feat, you can roll {@dice 1d6} instead of {@dice 1d4}.
Greater Mark of Detection.entries.1.name: Улучшенная интуиция
Greater Mark of Detection.entries.2.entr: When you use the Magical Detection benefit of your {@feat Mark of Detection|EFA} feat to cast {@spell See Invisibility|XPHB} without a spell slot, you can choose one creature you can see within 30 feet of yourself. That creature also gains the benefits of the spell for its duration.
Greater Mark of Detection.entries.2.name: Shared Detection
Greater Mark of Detection.name: Greater Mark of Detection
Greater Mark of Finding.entries.1.entrie: When you use the Hunter's Intuition benefit of your {@feat Mark of Finding|EFA} feat, you can roll {@dice 1d6} instead of {@dice 1d4}.
Greater Mark of Finding.entries.1.name: Улучшенная интуиция
Greater Mark of Finding.entries.2.entrie: When you use the Finder's Magic benefit of your {@feat Mark of Finding|EFA} to cast {@spell Hunter's Mark|XPHB} without a spell slot, the range of the spell is doubled, and you can modify the spell so that the target can't benefit from the {@condition Invisible|XPHB} condition for the duration.
Greater Mark of Finding.entries.2.name: Improved Finding
Greater Mark of Finding.name: Greater Mark of Finding
Greater Mark of Handling.entries.1.entri: When you use the Wild Intuition benefit of your {@feat Mark of Handling|EFA} feat, you can roll {@dice 1d6} instead of {@dice 1d4}.
Greater Mark of Handling.entries.1.name: Улучшенная интуиция
Greater Mark of Handling.entries.2.entri: While you are mounted, immediately after you hit a target within 5 feet of your mount with a melee attack roll, your mount can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to move up to its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} or take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action to make one attack only (your choice).
Greater Mark of Handling.entries.2.name: Improved Handling
Greater Mark of Handling.entries.3.entri: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you exert command over one Beast or Monstrosity you can see within 30 feet of yourself. The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw (DC 8 plus your Wisdom modifier and {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}) or have the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition until the start of your next turn. You can use this benefit a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Greater Mark of Handling.entries.3.name: Subdue Animal
Greater Mark of Handling.name: Greater Mark of Handling
Greater Mark of Healing.entries.1.entrie: When you use the Medical Intuition benefit of your {@feat Mark of Healing|EFA} feat, you can roll {@dice 1d6} instead of {@dice 1d4}.
Greater Mark of Healing.entries.1.name: Улучшенная интуиция
Greater Mark of Healing.entries.2.entrie: You can now use the Healing Touch benefit of your {@feat Mark of Healing|EFA} feat to cast {@spell Cure Wounds|XPHB} without using a spell slot a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. Additionally, when you cast {@spell Cure Wounds|XPHB} and roll dice to determine the number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} restored, you can treat any 1 or 2 on a roll as a 3.
Greater Mark of Healing.entries.2.name: Improved Healing
Greater Mark of Healing.name: Greater Mark of Healing
Greater Mark of Hospitality.entries.1.en: When you use the Ever Hospitable benefit of your {@feat Mark of Hospitality|EFA} feat, you can roll {@dice 1d6} instead of {@dice 1d4}.
Greater Mark of Hospitality.entries.2.en: When you cast {@spell Purify Food and Drink|XPHB}, you can modify the spell so that instead of its normal effect, each creature of your choice within 30 feet of you is refreshed. Each affected creature's {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} level is reduced by 1, and the creature gains {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} plus your Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma modifier (choose when you select this feat). Once you modify the spell with this benefit, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Greater Mark of Hospitality.name: Greater Mark of Hospitality
Greater Mark of Making.entries.1.entries: When you use the Artisan's Intuition benefit of your {@feat Mark of Making|EFA} feat, you can roll {@dice 1d6} instead of {@dice 1d4}.
Greater Mark of Making.entries.1.name: Улучшенная интуиция
Greater Mark of Making.entries.2.entries: When you use the Spellsmith benefit of your {@feat Mark of Making|EFA} feat to cast {@spell Magic Weapon|XPHB} without a spell slot, you cast the spell as its level 3 version.
Greater Mark of Making.entries.2.name: Improved Making
Greater Mark of Making.name: Greater Mark of Making
Greater Mark of Passage.entries.1.entrie: When you use the Intuitive Motion benefit of your {@feat Mark of Passage|EFA} feat, you can roll {@dice 1d6} instead of {@dice 1d4}.
Greater Mark of Passage.entries.1.name: Улучшенная интуиция
Greater Mark of Passage.entries.2.entrie: When you use the Magical Passage benefit of your {@feat Mark of Passage|EFA} feat to cast {@spell Misty Step|XPHB} without a spell slot, you can also choose up to two willing creatures you can see within 30 feet of yourself before you teleport. Each target can then take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to also teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space it can see.
Greater Mark of Passage.entries.2.name: Improved Passage
Greater Mark of Passage.name: Greater Mark of Passage
Greater Mark of Scribing.entries.1.entri: When you use the Gifted Scribe benefit of your {@feat Mark of Scribing|EFA} feat, you can roll {@dice 1d6} instead of {@dice 1d4}.
Greater Mark of Scribing.entries.1.name: Улучшенная интуиция
Greater Mark of Scribing.entries.2.entri: When you cast {@spell Comprehend Languages|XPHB}, you can modify the spell to encompass up to three willing creatures you can see within 30 feet of yourself. Each chosen creature also gains the benefits of the spell for the duration. In addition, for the duration of the spell, you and the chosen creatures can communicate telepathically with each other while within 1 mile of each other. Once you modify the spell with this benefit, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Greater Mark of Scribing.entries.2.name: Inspired Scribing
Greater Mark of Scribing.name: Greater Mark of Scribing
Greater Mark of Sentinel.entries.1.entri: When you use the Sentinel's Intuition benefit of your {@feat Mark of Sentinel|EFA} feat, you can roll {@dice 1d6} instead of {@dice 1d4}.
Greater Mark of Sentinel.entries.1.name: Улучшенная интуиция
Greater Mark of Sentinel.entries.2.entri: When you use the Vigilant Guardian benefit of your {@feat Mark of Sentinel|EFA} feat, you can also make one attack with a weapon or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} as part of that same {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}.
Greater Mark of Sentinel.entries.2.name: Improved Sentinel
Greater Mark of Sentinel.name: Greater Mark of Sentinel
Greater Mark of Shadow.entries.1.entries: When you use the Cunning Intuition benefit of your {@feat Mark of Shadow|EFA} feat, you can roll {@dice 1d6} instead of {@dice 1d4}.
Greater Mark of Shadow.entries.1.name: Улучшенная интуиция
Greater Mark of Shadow.entries.2.entries: When you use the Shape Shadows benefit of your {@feat Mark of Shadow|EFA} feat to cast {@spell Invisibility|XPHB} without a spell slot, you cast the spell as its level 3 version.
Greater Mark of Shadow.entries.2.name: Improved Shadow
Greater Mark of Shadow.name: Greater Mark of Shadow
When you use the Windwright's Intuition benefit of your {@feat Mark of Storm|EFA} feat, you can roll {@dice 1d6} instead of {@dice 1d4}.
Greater Mark of Storm.entries.1.name: Улучшенная интуиция
When you use the Storm Magic benefit of your {@feat Mark of Storm|EFA} feat to cast {@spell Gust of Wind|XPHB} without a spell slot, you also gain a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} of 60 feet for the duration of the spell.
Greater Mark of Storm.entries.2.name: Improved Storm
Greater Mark of Storm.name: Greater Mark of Storm
Greater Mark of Warding.entries.1.entrie: When you use the Warder's Intuition benefit of your {@feat Mark of Warding|EFA} feat, you can roll {@dice 1d6} instead of {@dice 1d4}.
Greater Mark of Warding.entries.1.name: Улучшенная интуиция
Greater Mark of Warding.entries.2.entrie: When a creature makes an attack roll against you or a creature you can see within 30 feet of yourself, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to impose {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on that roll. You can use this benefit a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Greater Mark of Warding.entries.2.name: Improved Warding
Greater Mark of Warding.name: Greater Mark of Warding
Guile of the Cloud Giant.entries.0: You've manifested the confounding magic emblematic of cloud giants, granting you the following benefits:
Guile of the Cloud Giant.entries.1.items: When a creature you can see hits you with an attack roll, you can use your reaction to give yourself resistance to that attack's damage. You then teleport to an unoccupied space that you can see within 30 feet of yourself. You can use this reaction a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.
Guile of the Cloud Giant.name: Guile of the Cloud Giant
Gunner.entries.0: You have a quick hand and keen eye when employing firearms, granting you the following benefits:
Gunner.entries.1.items.0: You gain proficiency with firearms (see "{@variantrule Firearms|DMG}" in the Dungeon Master's Guide).
Gunner.entries.1.items.1: You ignore the {@itemProperty LD|PHB|loading} property of firearms.
Gunner.entries.1.items.2: Being within 5 feet of a hostile creature doesn't impose disadvantage on your ranged attack rolls.
Gunner.name: Gunner
Harper Agent.entries.1.name: Thieves' Cant
Harper Agent.entries.2.entries.0: You gain proficiency with a {@item Musical Instrument|XPHB} of your choice.
Harper Agent.entries.2.name: Instrument Training
Harper Agent.entries.3.entries.0: When you take the {@action Help|XPHB} action to assist an ally's attack roll, the enemy you're distracting can be within 30 feet of you, rather than within 5 feet of you, provided the enemy can see or hear you.
Harper Agent.entries.3.name: Distracting Melody
Harper Agent.name: Harper Agent
Harper Teamwork.entries.1.entries.0: When you take the {@action Help|XPHB} action to assist an ally's attack roll against an enemy, that enemy also has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the first saving throw it makes before the start of your next turn.
Harper Teamwork.entries.1.name: Withering Wordplay
Harper Teamwork.entries.2.entries.0: If you succeed on a saving throw to end the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} or {@condition Paralyzed|XPHB} condition on yourself, you can choose one ally you can see within 30 feet of yourself that has the same condition. That condition immediately ends for that ally.
Harper Teamwork.entries.2.name: Inspiring Willpower
Harper Teamwork.name: Harper Teamwork
Healer.entries.1.entries.0: If you have a {@item Healer's Kit|XPHB}, you can expend one use of it and tend to a creature within 5 feet of yourself as a {@action Utilize|XPHB} action. That creature can expend one of its {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB}, and you then roll that die. The creature regains a number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the roll plus your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}.
Healer.entries.1.name: Battle Medic
Healer.entries.2.entries.0: Whenever you roll a die to determine the number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} you restore with a spell or with this feat's Battle Medic benefit, you can reroll the die if it rolls a 1, and you must use the new roll.
Healer.entries.2.name: Healing Rerolls
Healer.name: Healer
Heavily Armored.entries.1.entries.0: You gain training with Heavy armor.
Heavily Armored.entries.1.name: Владение доспехами
Heavily Armored.name: Heavily Armored
Heavy Armor Master.entries.1.entries.0: When you're hit by an attack while you're wearing Heavy armor, any Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage dealt to you by that attack is reduced by an amount equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}.
Heavy Armor Master.entries.1.name: Damage Reduction
Heavy Armor Master.name: Heavy Armor Master
Hulking Figure.entries.0: Either from extensive training or a natural build, you have a broad and formidable size for your species. You gain the following benefits.
Hulking Figure.entries.1.entries.0: Once per turn, you can deal an extra {@damage 1d4} Bludgeoning damage to a target you hit with an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}.
Hulking Figure.entries.1.name: Brutal
Hulking Figure.entries.2.entries.0: When you make a Charisma ({@skill Intimidation|XPHB}, {@skill Performance|XPHB}, or {@skill Persuasion|XPHB}) check, you can also add your Strength modifier to the roll.
Hulking Figure.entries.2.name: Intimidating
Hulking Figure.entries.3.entries.0: You count as one size larger (to a maximum size of Large) when determining your carrying capacity.
Hulking Figure.entries.3.name: Powerful
Hulking Figure.name: Hulking Figure
Infernal Constitution.entries.0: Fiendish blood runs strong in you, unlocking a resilience akin to that possessed by some fiends. You gain the following benefits:
Infernal Constitution.entries.1.items.0: You have resistance to cold and poison damage.
Infernal Constitution.entries.1.items.1: You have advantage on saving throws against being {@condition poisoned}.
Infernal Constitution.name: Infernal Constitution
Initiate of High Sorcery.entries.0: You've received training from magic-users affiliated with the Mages of High Sorcery.
Initiate of High Sorcery.entries.1: Choose one of the three moons of Krynn to influence your magic: the black moon, Nuitari; the red moon, Lunitari; or the white moon, Solinari. You learn one cantrip of your choice from the wizard spell list and two 1st-level spells based on the moon you choose, as specified in the Lunar Spells table.
Choose two from {@spell dissonant whispers}, {@spell false life}, {@spell hex}, and {@spell ray of sickness}
Choose two from {@spell color spray}, {@spell disguise self}, {@spell feather fall}, and {@spell longstrider}
Choose two from {@spell comprehend languages}, {@spell detect evil and good}, {@spell protection from evil and good}, and {@spell shield}
Initiate of High Sorcery.entries.3: You can cast each of the chosen 1st-level spells without a spell slot, and you must finish a long rest before you can cast them in this way again. You can also cast the spells using any spell slots you have.
Initiate of High Sorcery.entries.4: Your spellcasting ability for this feat's spells is Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma (choose when you select this feat).
Initiate of High Sorcery.name: Initiate of High Sorcery
Insightful Collector.entries.0: Your expeditions, transactions, and mercantile activities on behalf of the Augustine Trading Company have put you in contact with collectors of historical artifacts, relics, and tomes. You gain the following benefits.
Insightful Collector.entries.1.entries.0: You can take a {@action Study|XPHB} action to examine one magical object and learn its properties and how to use it without attuning to it or taking a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} in physical contact with it, but you don't learn any curse the item might bear. There is a cumulative 5% chance for every rarity above Common that you misidentify the nature and the power of the object.
Insightful Collector.entries.1.name: Object Intuition
Insightful Collector.entries.2.entries.0: You begin play in possession of a Common magic item. Work with your GM to decide on which object fits your character and the campaign.
Insightful Collector.entries.2.name: Rare Find
Insightful Collector.name: Insightful Collector
Inspiring Leader.entries.1.entries.0: When you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can give an inspiring performance: a speech, song, or dance. When you do so, choose up to six allies (which can include yourself) within 30 feet of yourself who witness the performance. The chosen creatures each gain {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to your character level plus the modifier of the ability you increased with this feat.
Inspiring Leader.entries.1.name: Bolstering Performance
Inspiring Leader.name: Inspiring Leader
Interception.entries.0: When a creature you can see hits another creature within 5 feet of you with an attack roll, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to reduce the damage dealt to the target by {@dice 1d10} plus your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}. You must be holding a {@item Shield|XPHB} or a Simple or Martial weapon to use this {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}.
Interception.name: Interception
Iron Gut.entries.0: You can eat like a hill giant and drink like a fish. Years of punishing your stomach and liver have led to a powerful fortitude. You gain the following benefits.
Iron Gut.entries.1.entries.0: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws you make to avoid or end the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition.
Iron Gut.entries.1.name: Inured to Poison
Iron Gut.entries.2.entries.0: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Wisdom ({@skill Survival|XPHB}) checks to forage for food.
Iron Gut.entries.2.name: Everything Looks Delicious
Iron Gut.entries.3.entries.0: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can expend one of your {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB}, roll the die and add your Constitution modifier, and regain a number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the roll's total. Once you use this benefit, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Iron Gut.entries.3.name: Quick to Recover
Iron Gut.name: Iron Gut
Keen Mind.entries.1.entries.0: Choose one of the following skills: {@skill Arcana|XPHB}, {@skill History|XPHB}, {@skill Investigation|XPHB}, {@skill Nature|XPHB}, or {@skill Religion|XPHB}. If you lack proficiency in the chosen skill, you gain proficiency in it, and if you already have proficiency in it, you gain {@variantrule Expertise|XPHB} in it.
Keen Mind.entries.1.name: Lore Knowledge
Keen Mind.entries.2.entries.0: You can take the {@action Study|XPHB} action as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.
Keen Mind.entries.2.name: Quick Study
Keen Mind.name: Keen Mind
Keenness of the Stone Giant.entries.0: You've manifested the physical talents emblematic of stone giants, granting you the following benefits:
Keenness of the Stone Giant.entries.1.it: You gain {@sense darkvision} with a range of 60 feet. If you already have {@sense darkvision} from another source, its range increases by 60 feet.

As a bonus action, you can take a rock and make a magical attack with it. The attack is a ranged spell attack with a range of 60 feet that uses the ability score you increased with this feat as the spellcasting ability. On a hit, the rock deals {@damage 1d10} force damage, and the target must succeed on a Strength saving throw (DC equals 8 + your proficiency bonus + the spellcasting ability modifier) or have the prone condition. You can use this bonus action a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.
Keenness of the Stone Giant.name: Keenness of the Stone Giant
Knight of the Crown.entries.0: You are a Knight of Solamnia aligned with the Order of the Crown, a group that extols the virtues of cooperation, loyalty, and obedience. You excel in group combat and gain these benefits:
Knight of the Crown.entries.1.items.0.en: As a bonus action, you can command one ally within 30 feet of yourself to attack. If that ally can see or hear you, they can immediately make one weapon attack as a reaction. If the attack hits, the ally can roll a {@dice d8} and add the number rolled as a bonus to the attack's damage roll. You can use this bonus action a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.
Knight of the Crown.entries.2.entries.0: Knights of Solamnia are only ever members of one of their organization's orders. In the course of their training, most knights begin as members of the Knights of the Crown and then move on to join other orders. Whether a character follows this path or another, they retain what they learned as a member of an order even if they join another. Characters can change what knightly order they're a part of, but they always have access to any Knight of Solamnia feats they've acquired.
Knight of the Crown.entries.2.name: Membership in Knightly Orders
Knight of the Crown.name: Knight of the Crown
Knight of the Rose.entries.0: You are a Knight of Solamnia aligned with the Order of the Rose, a group known for leadership, justice, and wisdom. Your resolve grants you these benefits:
Knight of the Rose.entries.1.items.0.ent: As a bonus action, you can encourage one creature you can see within 30 feet of yourself (you can choose yourself). If the target can see or hear you, the target gains temporary hit points equal to {@dice 1d8} + your proficiency bonus + the ability modifier of the ability score increased by this feat. You can use this bonus action a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.
Knight of the Rose.entries.2.entries.0: Knights of Solamnia are only ever members of one of their organization's orders. In the course of their training, most knights begin as members of the Knights of the Crown and then move on to join other orders. Whether a character follows this path or another, they retain what they learned as a member of an order even if they join another. Characters can change what knightly order they're a part of, but they always have access to any Knight of Solamnia feats they've acquired.
Knight of the Rose.entries.2.name: Membership in Knightly Orders
Knight of the Rose.name: Knight of the Rose
Knight of the Sword.entries.0: You are a Knight of Solamnia aligned with the Order of the Sword, a group devoted to heroism and courage. Bravery steels your spirit, granting you these benefits:
Knight of the Sword.entries.1.items.0.en: Once per turn, when you hit a creature with a weapon attack roll, you can attempt to frighten that target. The target must make a Wisdom saving throw (DC equals 8 + your proficiency bonus + the ability modifier of the score increased by this feat). On a failed save, the target is {@condition frightened} of you until the end of your next turn. On a successful save, the target has disadvantage on the next attack roll it makes before the end of its next turn. You can use this benefit a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.
Knight of the Sword.entries.2.entries.0: Knights of Solamnia are only ever members of one of their organization's orders. In the course of their training, most knights begin as members of the Knights of the Crown and then move on to join other orders. Whether a character follows this path or another, they retain what they learned as a member of an order even if they join another. Characters can change what knightly order they're a part of, but they always have access to any Knight of Solamnia feats they've acquired.
Knight of the Sword.entries.2.name: Membership in Knightly Orders
Knight of the Sword.name: Knight of the Sword
Light Bringer.entries.1.entries.0: You learn the {@spell Light|XPHB} spell and can cast it without Material components. If you already know that cantrip, you learn a different Cleric cantrip of your choice. The spell's spellcasting ability is the ability increased by this feat.
Light Bringer.entries.1.name: Sacred Magic
Light Bringer.entries.2.entries.0: When you cast {@spell Light|XPHB}, you can have the light from the spell be sunlight. Once you use this benefit, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Light Bringer.entries.2.name: Solar Luminance
Light Bringer.entries.3.entries.0: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} while within sunlight, you can expend one of your {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB}, roll the die, and regain a number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the roll. Once you use this benefit, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Light Bringer.entries.3.name: Sun's Healing
Light Bringer.name: Light Bringer
Lightly Armored.entries.1.entries.0: You gain training with Light armor and Shields.
Lightly Armored.entries.1.name: Владение доспехами
Lightly Armored.name: Lightly Armored
Lightning Caster.entries.0: Your rapid-fire style of casting cantrips allows you to weave magic with uncanny speed. You gain the following benefits.
Lightning Caster.entries.1.entries.0: When you cast a cantrip with a casting time of an action that targets a single creature, you can use a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to target a second creature within the cantrip's range.
Lightning Caster.entries.1.name: Dual Target
Lightning Caster.entries.2.entries.0: When you cast a spell as a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}, that spell doesn't expend a spell slot. Once you use this benefit, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Lightning Caster.entries.2.name: Immediate Response
Lightning Caster.name: Lightning Caster
Linguist.entries.0: You have studied languages and codes, gaining the following benefits:
Linguist.entries.1.items.0: You learn three languages of your choice.
Linguist.entries.1.items.1: You can ably create written ciphers. Others can't decipher a code you create unless you teach them, they succeed on an Intelligence check (DC equal to your Intelligence score + your proficiency bonus), or they use magic to decipher it.
Linguist.name: Linguist
Lordly Resolve.entries.1.entries.0: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, choose up to three creatures within 60 feet of yourself that can see you. Each target can immediately take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to right itself and end the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition, provided its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} isn't 0. Additionally, you bolster the targets' resolve, which lasts for 1 minute or until you have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition. While bolstered, a target can't be possessed or gain the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} or {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition; if a target is already possessed, {@condition Charmed|XPHB}, or {@condition Frightened|XPHB}, the target has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on any new saving throw against the relevant effect.
Lordly Resolve.entries.1.entries.1: Once you use this benefit, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Lordly Resolve.entries.1.name: Standard Bearer
Lordly Resolve.name: Lordly Resolve
Lords Alliance Agent.entries.1.entries.0: Once per turn when you score a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} against a creature, you can choose an ally within 30 feet of yourself who can see or hear you and who lacks {@variantrule Heroic Inspiration|XPHB}. That ally gains {@variantrule Heroic Inspiration|XPHB}.
Lords Alliance Agent.entries.1.name: Inspiring Strike
Lords Alliance Agent.entries.2.entries.0: When an enemy you can see deals damage to an ally of yours that is within 5 feet of you, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on your next attack roll against that enemy before the end of your next turn.
Lords Alliance Agent.entries.2.name: Reassert Honor
Lords Alliance Agent.name: Lords' Alliance Agent
Love Bites.entries.1.entries.0: Immediately after you damage a creature with a Melee weapon or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to give the target the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} condition until the start of your next turn or until you or your allies damage it. Once you use this benefit, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Love Bites.entries.1.name: Endearing Pain
Love Bites.name: Love Bites
Lucky.entries.1.entries.0: You have a number of Luck Points equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} and can spend the points on the benefits below. You regain your expended Luck Points when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Lucky.entries.1.name: Luck Points
Lucky.entries.2.entries.0: When you roll a {@dice d20} for a {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB}, you can spend 1 Luck Point to give yourself {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the roll.
Lucky.entries.2.name: Преимущество
Lucky.entries.3.entries.0: When a creature rolls a {@dice d20} for an attack roll against you, you can spend 1 Luck Point to impose {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on that roll.
Lucky.entries.3.name: Помеха
Lucky.name: Lucky
Mage Slayer.entries.1.entries.0: When you damage a creature that is {@status Concentration|XPHB|Concentrating}, it has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the saving throw it makes to maintain {@status Concentration|XPHB}.
Mage Slayer.entries.1.name: Concentration Breaker
Mage Slayer.entries.2.entries.0: If you fail an Intelligence, a Wisdom, or a Charisma saving throw, you can cause yourself to succeed instead. Once you use this benefit, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Mage Slayer.entries.2.name: Guarded Mind
Mage Slayer.name: Mage Slayer
Magic Initiate.entries.1.entries.0: You learn two cantrips of your choice from the {@filter Cleric|spells|level=0;1|class=Cleric}, {@filter Druid|spells|level=0;1|class=Druid}, or {@filter Wizard|spells|level=0;1|class=Wizard} spell list. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for this feat's spells (choose when you select this feat).
Magic Initiate.entries.1.name: Two Cantrips
Magic Initiate.entries.2.entries.0: Choose a level 1 spell from the same list you selected for this feat's cantrips. You always have that spell prepared. You can cast it once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also cast the spell using any spell slots you have.
Magic Initiate.entries.2.name: Level 1 Spell
Magic Initiate.entries.3.entries.0: Whenever you gain a new level, you can replace one of the spells you chose for this feat with a different spell of the same level from the chosen spell list.
Magic Initiate.entries.3.name: Spell Change
Magic Initiate.entries.4.entries.0: You can take this feat more than once, but you must choose a different spell list each time.
Magic Initiate.entries.4.name: Повторяемое
Magic Initiate.name: Magic Initiate
Mark of Detection.entries.1.entries.0: When you make an Intelligence ({@skill Investigation|XPHB}) or Wisdom ({@skill Insight|XPHB}) check, you can roll {@dice 1d4} and add the number rolled to the ability check.
Mark of Detection.entries.1.name: Deductive Intuition
Mark of Detection.entries.2.entries.0: You always have the {@spell Detect Magic|XPHB} and {@spell Detect Poison and Disease|XPHB} spells prepared. You can cast each spell once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also cast these spells using any spell slots you have of the appropriate level. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells (choose when you select this feat).
Mark of Detection.entries.2.entries.1: When you reach character level 3, you also always have the {@spell See Invisibility|XPHB} spell prepared and can cast it the same way.
Mark of Detection.entries.2.name: Magical Detection
Mark of Detection.entries.3.entries.0: If you have the Spellcasting or Pact Magic class feature, the spells on the Mark of Detection Spells table are added to that feature's spell list.
Mark of Detection.entries.3.name: Заклинания Метки
Mark of Detection.name: Mark of Detection
Mark of Finding.entries.1.entries.0: When you make a Wisdom ({@skill Perception|XPHB} or {@skill Survival|XPHB}) check, you can roll {@dice 1d4} and add the number rolled to the ability check.
Mark of Finding.entries.1.name: Hunter's Intuition
Mark of Finding.entries.2.entries.0: You always have the {@spell Hunter's Mark|XPHB} spell prepared. You can cast it once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also cast it using any spell slots you have of the appropriate level. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for this spell (choose when you select this feat).
Mark of Finding.entries.2.entries.1: When you reach character level 3, you also always have the {@spell Locate Object|XPHB} spell prepared and can cast it the same way.
Mark of Finding.entries.2.name: Finder's Magic
Mark of Finding.entries.3.entries.0: If you have the Spellcasting or Pact Magic class feature, the spells on the Mark of Finding Spells table are added to that feature's spell list.
Mark of Finding.entries.3.entries.1.rows: {@spell Locate Animals or Plants|XPHB}, {@spell Mind Spike|XPHB}
Mark of Finding.entries.3.name: Заклинания Метки
Mark of Finding.name: Mark of Finding
Mark of Handling.entries.1.entries.0: When you make an Intelligence ({@skill Nature|XPHB}) or Wisdom ({@skill Animal Handling|XPHB}) check, you can roll {@dice 1d4} and add the number rolled to the ability check.
Mark of Handling.entries.1.name: Wild Intuition
Mark of Handling.entries.2.entries.0: You always have the {@spell Animal Friendship|XPHB} and {@spell Speak with Animals|XPHB} spells prepared. You can cast each spell once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also cast these spells using any spell slots you have. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells (choose when you select this feat).
Mark of Handling.entries.2.name: Primal Connection
Mark of Handling.entries.3.entries.0: When you reach character level 3, you can target a Monstrosity when you cast {@spell Animal Friendship|XPHB} or {@spell Speak with Animals|XPHB} if the creature's Intelligence score is 3 or lower.
Mark of Handling.entries.3.name: Monstrous Connections
Mark of Handling.entries.4.entries.0: If you have the Spellcasting or Pact Magic class feature, the spells on the Mark of Handling Spells table are added to that feature's spell list.
Mark of Handling.entries.4.name: Заклинания Метки
Mark of Handling.name: Mark of Handling
Mark of Healing.entries.1.entries.0: When you make a Wisdom ({@skill Medicine|XPHB}) check or an ability check using a {@item Herbalism Kit|XPHB}, you can roll {@dice 1d4} and add the number rolled to the ability check.
Mark of Healing.entries.1.name: Medical Intuition
Mark of Healing.entries.2.entries.0: You always have the {@spell Cure Wounds|XPHB} spell prepared. You can cast it once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also cast it using any spell slots you have of the appropriate level. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for this spell (choose when you select this feat).
Mark of Healing.entries.2.entries.1: When you reach character level 3, you also always have the {@spell Lesser Restoration|XPHB} spell prepared and can cast it the same way.
Mark of Healing.entries.2.name: Healing Touch
Mark of Healing.entries.3.entries.0: If you have the Spellcasting or Pact Magic class feature, the spells on the Mark of Healing Spells table are added to that feature's spell list.
Mark of Healing.entries.3.entries.1.rows: {@spell Aura of Vitality|XPHB}, {@spell Mass Healing Word|XPHB}
{@spell Aura of Life|XPHB}, {@spell Aura of Purity|XPHB}
Mark of Healing.entries.3.name: Заклинания Метки
Mark of Healing.name: Mark of Healing
Mark of Hospitality.entries.1.entries.0: When you make a Charisma ({@skill Persuasion|XPHB}) check or an ability check using {@item Brewer's Supplies|XPHB} or {@item Cook's Utensils|XPHB}, you can roll {@dice 1d4} and add the number rolled to the ability check.
Mark of Hospitality.entries.1.name: Ever Hospitable
Mark of Hospitality.entries.2.entries.0: You always have the {@spell Purify Food and Drink|XPHB} and {@spell Unseen Servant|XPHB} spells prepared. You can cast each spell once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also cast these spells using any spell slots you have of the appropriate level. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells (choose when you select this feat).
Mark of Hospitality.entries.2.entries.1: When you reach character level 3, you also always have the {@spell Calm Emotions|XPHB} spell prepared and can cast it the same way
Mark of Hospitality.entries.2.name: Innkeeper's Magic
Mark of Hospitality.entries.3.entries.0: If you have the Spellcasting or Pact Magic class feature, the spells on the Mark of Hospitality Spells table are added to that feature's spell list.
{@spell Create Food and Water|XPHB}, {@spell Leomund's Tiny Hut|XPHB}
{@spell Aura of Purity|XPHB}, {@spell Mordenkainen's Private Sanctum|XPHB}
Mark of Hospitality.entries.3.name: Заклинания Метки
Mark of Hospitality.name: Mark of Hospitality
Mark of Making.entries.1.entries.0: When you make an Intelligence ({@skill Arcana|XPHB}) check or an ability check using {@item Artisan's Tools|XPHB}, you can roll {@dice 1d4} and add the number rolled to the ability check.
Mark of Making.entries.1.name: Artisan's Intuition
Mark of Making.entries.2.entries.0: You know the {@spell Mending|XPHB} cantrip. You also always have the {@spell Magic Weapon|XPHB} spell prepared. You can cast it once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also cast it using any spell slots you have. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells (choose when you select this feat).
Mark of Making.entries.2.name: Spellsmith
Mark of Making.entries.3.entries.0: If you have the Spellcasting or Pact Magic class feature, the spells on the Mark of Making Spells table are added to that feature's spell list.
Mark of Making.entries.3.name: Заклинания Метки
Mark of Making.name: Mark of Making
Mark of Passage.entries.1.name: Courier's Speed
Mark of Passage.entries.2.entries.0: When you make a Strength ({@skill Athletics|XPHB}) or Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics|XPHB}) check, you can roll {@dice 1d4} and add the number rolled to the ability check.
Mark of Passage.entries.2.name: Intuitive Motion
Mark of Passage.entries.3.entries.0: You always have the {@spell Misty Step|XPHB} spell prepared. You can cast it once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also cast it using any spell slots you have. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for this spell (choose when you select this feat).
Mark of Passage.entries.3.name: Magical Passage
Mark of Passage.entries.4.entries.0: If you have the Spellcasting or Pact Magic class feature, the spells on the Mark of Passage Spells table are added to that feature's spell list.
Mark of Passage.entries.4.name: Заклинания Метки
Mark of Passage.name: Mark of Passage
Mark of Scribing.entries.1.entries.0: When you make an Intelligence ({@skill History|XPHB}) check or an ability check using {@item Calligrapher's Supplies|XPHB}, you can roll {@dice 1d4} and add the number rolled to the ability check.
Mark of Scribing.entries.1.name: Gifted Scribe
Mark of Scribing.entries.2.entries.0: You know the {@spell Message|XPHB} cantrip. You also always have the {@spell Comprehend Languages|XPHB} spell prepared. You can cast it once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also cast it using any spell slots you have of the appropriate level. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for this spell (choose when you select this feat).
Mark of Scribing.entries.2.entries.1: When you reach character level 3, you also always have the {@spell Magic Mouth|XPHB} spell prepared and can cast it the same way.
Mark of Scribing.entries.2.name: Scribe's Insight
Mark of Scribing.entries.3.entries.0: If you have the Spellcasting or Pact Magic class feature, the spells on the Mark of Scribing Spells table are added to that feature's spell list.
Mark of Scribing.entries.3.name: Заклинания Метки
Mark of Scribing.name: Mark of Scribing
Mark of Sentinel.entries.1.entries.0: When you make a Wisdom ({@skill Insight|XPHB} or {@skill Perception|XPHB}) check, you can roll {@dice 1d4} and add the number rolled to the ability check.
Mark of Sentinel.entries.1.name: Sentinel's Intuition
Mark of Sentinel.entries.2.entries.0: You always have the {@spell Shield|XPHB} spell prepared. You can cast it once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also cast it using any spell slots you have. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for this spell (choose when you select this feat).
Mark of Sentinel.entries.2.name: Guardian's Shield
Mark of Sentinel.entries.3.entries.0: When a creature you can see within 5 feet of you is hit by an attack roll, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to swap places with that creature, and you are hit by the attack instead. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} and regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Mark of Sentinel.entries.3.name: Vigilant Guardian
Mark of Sentinel.entries.4.entries.0: If you have the Spellcasting or Pact Magic class feature, the spells on the Mark of Sentinel Spells table are added to that feature's spell list.
Mark of Sentinel.entries.4.name: Заклинания Метки
Mark of Sentinel.name: Mark of Sentinel
Mark of Shadow.entries.1.entries.0: When you make a Dexterity ({@skill Stealth|XPHB}) or Charisma ({@skill Performance|XPHB}) check, you can roll {@dice 1d4} and add the number rolled to the ability check.
Mark of Shadow.entries.1.name: Cunning Intuition
Mark of Shadow.entries.2.entries.0: You know the {@spell Minor Illusion|XPHB} cantrip. You also always have the {@spell Invisibility|XPHB} spell prepared. You can cast it once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also cast it using any spell slots you have of the appropriate level. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells (choose when you select this feat).
Mark of Shadow.entries.2.name: Shape Shadows
Mark of Shadow.entries.3.entries.0: If you have the Spellcasting or Pact Magic class feature, the spells on the Mark of Shadow Spells table are added to that feature's spell list.
Mark of Shadow.entries.3.name: Заклинания Метки
Mark of Shadow.name: Mark of Shadow
Mark of Storm.entries.1.entries.0: When you make a Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics|XPHB}) check or an ability check using {@item Navigator's Tools|XPHB}, you can roll {@dice 1d4} and add the number rolled to the ability check.
Mark of Storm.entries.1.name: Windwright's Intuition
Mark of Storm.entries.2.entries.0: You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Lightning damage.
Mark of Storm.entries.2.name: Storm's Boon
Mark of Storm.entries.3.entries.0: You know the {@spell Thunderclap|XPHB} cantrip. When you reach character level 3, you also always have the {@spell Gust of Wind|XPHB} spell prepared. You can cast it once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also cast it using any spell slots you have of the appropriate level. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells (choose when you select this feat).
Mark of Storm.entries.3.name: Storm Magic
Mark of Storm.entries.4.entries.0: If you have the Spellcasting or Pact Magic class feature, the spells on the Mark of Storm Spells table are added to that feature's spell list.
Mark of Storm.entries.4.name: Заклинания Метки
Mark of Storm.name: Mark of Storm
Mark of Warding.entries.1.entries.0: When you make an Intelligence ({@skill Investigation|XPHB}) check or an ability check using {@item Thieves' Tools|XPHB}, you can roll {@dice 1d4} and add the number rolled to the ability check.
Mark of Warding.entries.1.name: Warder's Intuition
Mark of Warding.entries.2.entries.0: You always have the {@spell Alarm|XPHB} and {@spell Mage Armor|XPHB} spells prepared. You can cast each spell once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Mark of Warding.entries.2.entries.1: You can also cast these spells using any spell slots you have of the appropriate level. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells (choose when you select this feat).
Mark of Warding.entries.2.entries.2: When you reach character level 3, you also always have the {@spell Arcane Lock|XPHB} spell prepared and can cast it the same way.
Mark of Warding.entries.2.name: Wards and Seals
Mark of Warding.entries.3.entries.0: If you have the Spellcasting or Pact Magic class feature, the spells on the Mark of Warding Spells table are added to that feature's spell list.
Mark of Warding.entries.3.entries.1.rows: {@spell Leomund's Secret Chest|XPHB}, {@spell Mordenkainen's Faithful Hound|XPHB}
Mark of Warding.entries.3.name: Заклинания Метки
Mark of Warding.name: Mark of Warding
Martial Adept.entries.0: You have martial training that allows you to perform special combat maneuvers. You gain the following benefits:
Martial Adept.entries.1.items.0: You learn two {@filter maneuvers|optionalfeatures|feature type=MV:B} of your choice from among those available to the {@class fighter|phb|Battle Master|Battle Master|phb|2-0} archetype in the fighter class. If a maneuver you use requires your target to make a saving throw to resist the maneuver's effects, the saving throw DC equals 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Strength or Dexterity modifier (your choice).
Martial Adept.entries.1.items.1: You gain one superiority die, which is a {@dice d6} (this die is added to any superiority dice you have from another source). This die is used to fuel your maneuvers. A superiority die is expended when you use it. You regain your expended superiority dice when you finish a short or long rest.
Martial Adept.name: Martial Adept
Martial Weapon Training.entries.1.entrie: You gain proficiency with {@filter Martial weapons|items|type=martial weapon}.
Martial Weapon Training.entries.1.name: Weapon Proficiency
Martial Weapon Training.name: Martial Weapon Training
Medician of the Morbus Doctore.entries.0: You are a master of the medical sciences because of your association with a medical institute. You gain the following benefits.
Medician of the Morbus Doctore.entries.1: When you use the Blood and Bone benefit of the Triage Expert feat, the creature can spend up to three {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB} instead of one.
Medician of the Morbus Doctore.entries.2: When you use the Blood and Bone benefit of the Triage Expert feat, the creature can spend three {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB} to end one of the following conditions on itself instead of regaining {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}: {@condition Blinded|XPHB}, {@condition Deafened|XPHB}, {@condition Paralyzed|XPHB}, {@condition Poisoned|XPHB}, or {@condition Stunned|XPHB}.
Alternatively, you can cure one {@variantrule Grievous Wound|GrimHollowPG24} affecting the creature (see Grim Hollow {@variantrule Campaign|XPHB} Guide).
Medician of the Morbus Doctore.name: Medician of the Morbus Doctore
Medium Armor Master.entries.1.entries.0: While you're wearing Medium armor, you can add 3, rather than 2 to your AC if you have a Dexterity score of 16 or higher.
Medium Armor Master.entries.1.name: Dexterous Wearer
Medium Armor Master.name: Medium Armor Master
Metamagic Adept.entries.0: You've learned how to exert your will on your spells to alter how they function:
Metamagic Adept.entries.1.items.0: You learn two {@filter Metamagic|optionalfeatures|Feature Type=MM} options of your choice from the sorcerer class. You can use only one Metamagic option on a spell when you cast it, unless the option says otherwise. Whenever you reach a level that grants the Ability Score Improvement feature, you can replace one of these Metamagic options with another one from the sorcerer class.
Metamagic Adept.entries.1.items.1: You gain 2 sorcery points to spend on Metamagic (these points are added to any sorcery points you have from another source but can be used only on Metamagic). You regain all spent sorcery points when you finish a long rest.
Metamagic Adept.name: Metamagic Adept
Mobile Combatant.entries.1.entries.0: When you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action, your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases by 10 feet and {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks} have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} against you until the end of your turn.
Mobile Combatant.entries.1.name: Slippery
Mobile Combatant.name: Mobile Combatant
Moderately Armored.entries.1.entries.0: You gain training with Medium armor.
Moderately Armored.entries.1.name: Владение доспехами
Moderately Armored.name: Moderately Armored
Mounted Combatant.entries.1.entries.0: While {@book mounted|XPHB|1|Mounted Combat}, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attack rolls against any unmounted creature within 5 feet of your mount that is at least one size smaller than the mount.
Mounted Combatant.entries.1.name: Mounted Strike
Mounted Combatant.entries.2.entries.0: If your mount is subjected to an effect that allows it to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, it instead takes no damage if it succeeds on the saving throw and only half damage if it fails. For your mount to gain this benefit, you must be riding it, and neither of you can have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition.
Mounted Combatant.entries.2.name: Leap Aside
Mounted Combatant.entries.3.entries.0: While {@book mounted|XPHB|1|Mounted Combat}, you can force an attack that hits your mount to hit you instead if you don't have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition.
Mounted Combatant.entries.3.name: Veer
Mounted Combatant.name: Mounted Combatant
Musician.entries.1.entries.0: You gain proficiency with three Musical Instruments of your choice.
Musician.entries.1.name: Instrument Training
Musician.entries.2.entries.0: As you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can play a song on a {@item Musical Instrument|XPHB} with which you have proficiency and give {@variantrule Heroic Inspiration|XPHB} to allies who hear the song. The number of allies you can affect in this way equals your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}.
Musician.entries.2.name: Encouraging Song
Musician.name: Musician
Mythal Touched.entries.0.entries.0: If a spell attack hits you or you fail a saving throw against a spell, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to roll on the Mythal-Touched {@action Magic|XPHB} table to create a magical effect. If an effect requires a saving throw, the DC equals 8 plus the modifier of the ability increased by this feat and your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}.
Mythal Touched.entries.0.entries.1: You can use this benefit a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
You and each creature within 15 feet of you make a Dexterity saving throw, taking Force damage equal to {@dice 1d8} times the level of the triggering spell on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.
You and the triggering spell's caster form a telepathic link for 1 hour.
Gravity is reversed in a 15-foot-radius, 60-foot-tall {@variantrule Cylinder [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cylinder} centered on you for 1 minute, per the {@spell Reverse Gravity|XPHB} spell.
You and the triggering spell's caster each make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the creature has the {@condition Stunned|XPHB} condition until the end of its next turn.
You gain a +2 bonus to AC for 1 minute, potentially turning the triggering spell into a miss if it was a spell attack.

Any flammable, nonmagical object within 10 feet of the triggering spell's caster that isn't being worn or carried by another creature bursts into flame, takes {@damage 1d4} Fire damage, and is burning.

The triggering spell dissipates with no effect, and the action, {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, or {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} used to cast it is wasted. If that spell was cast with a spell slot, the slot isn't expended.
Mythal Touched.entries.0.name: Mythal Ward
Mythal Touched.name: Mythal Touched
Nimble Physique.entries.0: You are small and thin for your species. You have a mysterious and consistent ability to avoid danger. You gain the following benefits:
Nimble Physique.entries.1.items.0.entrie: While you aren't wearing armor or wielding a {@item Shield|XPHB}, you can take the {@action Dodge|XPHB} action as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.
Nimble Physique.entries.1.items.0.name: Dodgy
Nimble Physique.entries.1.items.1.entrie: While you are {@condition Grappled|XPHB} or {@condition Restrained|XPHB}, your attacks don't have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} and attacks against you don't have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB}.
Nimble Physique.entries.1.items.1.name: Slippery
Nimble Physique.name: Nimble Physique
Observant.entries.1.entries.0: Choose one of the following skills: {@skill Insight|XPHB}, {@skill Investigation|XPHB}, or {@skill Perception|XPHB}. If you lack proficiency with the chosen skill, you gain proficiency in it, and if you already have proficiency in it, you gain {@variantrule Expertise|XPHB} in it.
Observant.entries.1.name: Keen Observer
Observant.entries.2.entries.0: You can take the {@action Search|XPHB} action as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.
Observant.entries.2.name: Quick Search
Observant.name: Observant
Opportunist.entries.1.entries.0: Whenever you make an attack as part of a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}, you gain a +2 bonus to the attack and damage rolls.
Opportunist.entries.1.name: Exploit Weakness
Opportunist.name: Opportunist
Orcish Fury.entries.0: Your fury burns tirelessly. You gain the following benefits:
Orcish Fury.entries.1.items.0: When you hit with an attack using a {@filter simple or martial weapon|items|source=phb|category=basic|type=martial weapon;simple weapon}, you can roll one of the weapon's damage dice an additional time and add it as extra damage of the weapon's damage type. Once you use this ability, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.
Orcish Fury.entries.1.items.1: Immediately after you use your Relentless Endurance trait, you can use your reaction to make one weapon attack.
Orcish Fury.name: Orcish Fury
Orders Resilience.entries.1.entries.0: When you have the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition, you can right yourself with only 5 feet of movement.
Orders Resilience.entries.1.name: Resurge
Orders Resilience.entries.2.entries.0: If you are within 5 feet of an ally that doesn't have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition, you and that ally have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Strength saving throws. You can't use this benefit while you have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition.
Orders Resilience.entries.2.name: Stronger Together
Orders Resilience.name: Order's Resilience
Outlands Envoy.entries.0: You have spent significant time in Sigil or the Outlands, the crossroads of the multiverse. Being steeped in converging planar energies grants you these benefits:
Outlands Envoy.entries.1.items.0.entries: You learn the {@spell misty step} and {@spell tongues} spells. You can cast each spell once using this feat without a spell slot, and you must finish a long rest before you can cast that spell in this way again. When you cast {@spell tongues} using this feat, you require no material components. You can also cast these spells using spell slots you have of the appropriate level. The spells' spellcasting ability is the one chosen when you gained the Scion of the Outer Planes feat.
Outlands Envoy.entries.1.items.0.name: Crossroads Emissary
Outlands Envoy.name: Outlands Envoy
Piercer.entries.1.entries.0: Once per turn, when you hit a creature with an attack that deals Piercing damage, you can reroll one of the attack's damage dice, and you must use the new roll.
Piercer.entries.1.name: Puncture
Piercer.entries.2.entries.0: When you score a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} that deals Piercing damage to a creature, you can roll one additional damage die when determining the extra Piercing damage the target takes.
Piercer.entries.2.name: Enhanced Critical
Piercer.name: Piercer
Planar Wanderer.entries.0: You can draw on the forces of the multiverse to survive cosmic extremes and to traverse its infinite realms, granting you these benefits:
Planar Wanderer.entries.1.items.0.entrie: When you finish a long rest, you gain resistance to either acid, cold, or fire damage (your choice) until you finish your next long rest.
Planar Wanderer.entries.1.items.0.name: Planar Adaptation
Planar Wanderer.entries.1.items.1.entrie: Your experience with portals allows you to open them without a portal key. As an action, you can concentrate on a portal you're aware of that is within 5 feet of yourself and make a {@dc 20} Intelligence ({@skill Arcana}) check. On a failed check, you take {@damage 3d8} psychic damage and can't use this benefit on that portal again until you finish a long rest. On a successful check, you can force the portal open or closed for 1 hour. For that duration, the portal doesn't respond to its portal key unless a creature employing the key succeeds on a {@dc 20} Intelligence ({@skill Arcana}) check as an action.
Planar Wanderer.entries.1.items.1.name: Portal Cracker
Planar Wanderer.entries.1.items.2.entrie: You know the direction to the last planar portal you used while you and the portal are on the same plane of existence. Moreover, as an action, you can detect the location of any portals within 30 feet of you that aren't behind {@quickref Cover||3||total cover}. Once you detect a portal with this action, you can't use the action again until you finish a long rest.
Planar Wanderer.entries.1.items.2.name: Portal Sense
Planar Wanderer.name: Planar Wanderer
Poisoner.entries.1.entries.0: When you make a damage roll that deals Poison damage, it ignores {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Poison damage.
Poisoner.entries.1.name: Potent Poison
Poisoner.entries.2.entries.0: You gain proficiency with the {@item Poisoner's Kit|XPHB}. With 1 hour of work using such a kit and expending 50 GP worth of materials, you can create a number of poison doses equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can apply a poison dose to a weapon or piece of ammunition. Once applied, the poison retains its potency for 1 minute or until you deal damage with the poisoned item, whichever is shorter. When a creature takes damage from the poisoned item, that creature must succeed on a Constitution saving throw ({@dc 8} plus the modifier of the ability increased by this feat and your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}) or take {@damage 2d8} Poison damage and have the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition until the end of your next turn.
Poisoner.entries.2.name: Brew Poison
Poisoner.name: Poisoner
Polearm Master.entries.1.entries.0: Immediately after you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action and attack with a {@item Quarterstaff|XPHB}, a {@item Spear|XPHB}, or a {@filter weapon that has the Heavy and Reach properties|items|type=melee weapon;ranged weapon|property=heavy;reach=sand}, you can use a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to make a melee attack with the opposite end of the weapon. The weapon deals Bludgeoning damage, and the weapon's damage die for this attack is a {@dice d4}.
Polearm Master.entries.1.name: Pole Strike
Polearm Master.entries.2.entries.0: While you're holding a {@item Quarterstaff|XPHB}, a {@item Spear|XPHB}, or a {@filter weapon that has the Heavy and Reach properties|items|type=melee weapon;ranged weapon|property=heavy;reach=sand}, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to make one melee attack against a creature that enters the reach you have with that weapon.
Polearm Master.entries.2.name: Reactive Strike
Polearm Master.name: Polearm Master
Potent Dragonmark.entries.1.entries.0: You always have the spells on your Spells of the Mark list (if any) prepared.
Potent Dragonmark.entries.1.name: Dragonmark Preparation
Potent Dragonmark.entries.2.entries.0: You have one extra spell slot to cast the spells granted by your Dragonmark feat. The spell slot's level is half your level (round up), to a maximum of level 5. You regain the expended slot when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can use this spell slot to cast only a spell you have prepared because of your Dragonmark feat or the Dragonmark Preparation benefit of this feat.
Potent Dragonmark.entries.2.name: Dragonmark Spellcasting
Potent Dragonmark.name: Potent Dragonmark
Prodigy.entries.0: You have a knack for learning new things. You gain the following benefits:
Prodigy.entries.1.items.0: You gain one {@table skills|phb|skill proficiency} of your choice, one {@filter tool proficiency|items|type=tool;artisan's tools} of your choice, and fluency in one language of your choice.
Prodigy.entries.1.items.1: Choose one skill in which you have proficiency. You gain expertise with that skill, which means your proficiency bonus is doubled for any ability check you make with it. The skill you choose must be one that isn't already benefiting from a feature, such as Expertise, that doubles your proficiency bonus.
Prodigy.name: Prodigy
Prone Defense.entries.1.entries.0: When you have the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition, you don't have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on attack rolls. {@action Attack|XPHB} roll against you doesn't have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} because of the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition.
Prone Defense.entries.1.name: Defensive
Prone Defense.entries.2.entries.0: When you have the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition, you can right yourself with only 5 feet of movement.
Prone Defense.entries.2.name: Hop Up
Prone Defense.name: Prone Defense
Protection.entries.0: When a creature you can see attacks a target other than you that is within 5 feet of you, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to interpose your {@item Shield|XPHB} if you're holding one. You impose {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the triggering attack roll and all other attack rolls against the target until the start of your next turn if you remain within 5 feet of the target.
Protection.name: Protection
Purple Dragon Commandant.entries.0.entri: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you bolster one ally you can see within 30 feet. The ally gains {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to {@dice 2d6} plus the modifier of the ability score increased by this feat. You can take this {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and you regain all uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Purple Dragon Commandant.entries.0.name: Encourage Ally
Purple Dragon Commandant.entries.1.entri: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attack rolls while {@status Bloodied|XPHB}.
Purple Dragon Commandant.entries.1.name: Last Stand
Purple Dragon Commandant.name: Purple Dragon Commandant
Purple Dragon Rook.entries.1.entries.0: You gain proficiency in one of the following skills: {@skill Insight|XPHB}, {@skill Performance|XPHB}, or {@skill Persuasion|XPHB}.
Purple Dragon Rook.entries.1.name: Entreat
Purple Dragon Rook.entries.2.entries.0: When you roll {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} and don't have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition, you can choose a number of creatures equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} that you can see within 30 feet of yourself. Those creatures gain {@variantrule Heroic Inspiration|XPHB}.
Purple Dragon Rook.entries.2.entries.1: Once you use this benefit, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Purple Dragon Rook.entries.2.name: Rallying Cry
Purple Dragon Rook.name: Purple Dragon Rook
Putrefy.entries.1.entries.0: When you make a damage roll that deals Necrotic damage, you can cause one creature taking that damage to have the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition until the start of your next turn. Once you use this benefit, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Putrefy.entries.1.name: Necrosis
Putrefy.name: Putrefy
Quicksmithing.entries.0: You have mastered the art of on-the-fly invention, improvement, and jury-rigging. You can use your talents to create immediate, short-term magical effects similar to spells, given time and an adequate supply of aether.
Quicksmithing.entries.1: When you choose this feat, you master two magical effects, each of which recreates the effect of a 1st-level spell that has the ritual tag. These spells can come from any class list, but Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for them.
Quicksmithing.entries.2: If you come across a schematic geared toward quicksmithing or study with another quicksmith, you might be able to add another spell to the effects you have mastered. The spell's level can be no higher than half your level (rounded up), and it must have the ritual tag. The process of mastering the spell takes 2 hours per level of the spell, and costs 50 gp per level. The cost represents aether you use as you experiment with the spell effect to master it.
Quicksmithing.entries.3: In addition, you have proficiency with {@item artisan's tools|PHB} (quicksmith's tools). Using those tools, you can spend 1 hour and 10 gp worth of materials to construct a Tiny clockwork device (AC 5, 1 hp). The device ceases to function after 24 hours unless you spend 1 hour repairing it to keep it functioning. You can use your action to dismantle the device, at which point you can reclaim the materials used to create it. You can have up to three such devices active at a time.
Quicksmithing.entries.4: When you create a device, choose one of the following options:
Quicksmithing.entries.5.entries.0: This toy is a clockwork animal, monster, or person, such as a frog, mouse, bird, dragon, or soldier. When placed on the ground, the toy moves 5 feet across the ground on each of your turns in a random direction. It makes noises as appropriate to the creature it represents.
Quicksmithing.entries.5.name: Clockwork Toy
Quicksmithing.entries.6.entries.0: This device produces a miniature flame, which you can use to light a candle, torch, or campfire. Using the device requires your action.
Quicksmithing.entries.6.name: Fire Starter
Quicksmithing.entries.7.entries.0: When opened, this music box plays a single song at a moderate volume. The box stops playing when it reaches the song's end or when it is closed.
Quicksmithing.entries.7.name: Music Box
Quicksmithing.name: Quicksmithing
Rebuke.entries.1.entries.0: When you make a damage roll that deals Radiant damage, you can cause one Huge or smaller creature taking the damage to have the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition. Once you use this benefit, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Rebuke.entries.1.name: Radiant Strike
Rebuke.name: Rebuke
Resilient.entries.1.entries.0: You gain saving throw proficiency with the chosen ability.
Resilient.entries.1.name: Saving Throw Proficiency
Resilient.name: Прочный
Resolution of the Syndicate.entries.0: Membership in the Ebon Syndicate provides perks—one of which is the motivation that comes with the understanding that the Syndicate kills those who fail too often. You gain the following benefits.
Resolution of the Syndicate.entries.1.en: On your first turn after an {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} roll, add {@dice 1d4} to the first damage roll you make. This extra damage increases to {@dice 2d4} at character level 9, and to {@dice 4d4} at character level 16.
Resolution of the Syndicate.entries.2.en: Your {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximum increases by an amount equal to your character level when you gain this feat. Whenever you gain a character level thereafter, your {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximum increases by an additional 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point}.
Resolution of the Syndicate.name: Resolution of the Syndicate
Revenant Blade.entries.0: You are descended from a master of the {@item double-bladed scimitar|ERLW}, and some of that mastery has passed on to you. You gain the following benefits:
Revenant Blade.entries.1.items.0: While you are holding a double-bladed scimitar with two hands, you gain a +1 bonus to Armor Class.
Revenant Blade.entries.1.items.1: A double-bladed scimitar has the {@itemProperty F|PHB|finesse} property when you wield it.
Revenant Blade.name: Revenant Blade
Righteous Heritor.entries.0: You can channel cosmic forces of good to gain these benefits:
Righteous Heritor.entries.1.items.0.entr: When you or a creature within 30 feet of you takes damage, you can use your reaction to reduce that damage by {@dice 1d10} + your proficiency bonus. You can use this benefit a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.
Righteous Heritor.entries.1.items.0.name: Soothe Pain
Righteous Heritor.name: Righteous Heritor
Ritual Caster.entries.1.entries.0: Choose a number of level 1 spells equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} that have the {@filter Ritual tag|spells|level=1|components & miscellaneous=ritual}. You always have those spells prepared, and you can cast them with any spell slots you have. The spells' spellcasting ability is the ability increased by this feat. Whenever your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} increases thereafter, you can add an additional level 1 spell with the {@variantrule Ritual|XPHB} tag to the spells always prepared with this feature.
Ritual Caster.entries.1.name: Ritual Spells
Ritual Caster.entries.2.entries.0: With this benefit, you can cast a {@variantrule Ritual|XPHB} spell that you have prepared using its regular casting time rather than the extended time for a {@variantrule Ritual|XPHB}. Doing so doesn't require a spell slot. Once you cast the spell in this way, you can't use this benefit again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Ritual Caster.entries.2.name: Quick Ritual
Ritual Caster.name: Ritual Caster
Rune Shaper.entries.0: You've studied the magic of Giant runes, granting you the following benefits:
Rune Shaper.entries.1.items.0.entries.0: You learn the {@spell comprehend languages} spell. You can cast this spell without expending a spell slot, and you must finish a long rest before you can cast it in this way again. You can also cast this spell using any spell slots you have.
Rune Shaper.entries.1.items.0.name: Comprehend Languages
Rune Shaper.entries.1.items.1.entries.0: You know a number of runes equal to half your proficiency bonus (rounded down), chosen from the Rune Spells table. Whenever you finish a long rest, you can inscribe each rune you know onto one nonmagical weapon, armor, piece of clothing, or other object you touch. You temporarily learn the 1st-level spells that correspond to the runes you inscribed, as specified on the Rune Spells table, and you know those spells until you finish a long rest, when the runes fade. While you are wearing or carrying any rune-marked object, you can cast the spells associated with those runes using any spell slots you have.
Rune Shaper.entries.1.items.1.entries.1: You can also invoke a rune inscribed on an object you are wearing or carrying and cast its associated spell without expending a spell slot or using material components. Once you cast the spell in this way, you can't do so again until you finish a long rest. Your spellcasting ability for this feat is Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma (choose when you select this feat).
Rune Shaper.entries.1.items.1.entries.2: Each time you gain a level, you can replace one of the runes you know with another one from the Rune Spells table.
Rune Shaper.entries.1.items.1.name: Rune Magic
Rune Shaper.name: Rune Shaper
Sangromantic Initiate.entries.0: You have become adept enough with weaving blood magic to mitigate some of the harm of Sangromancy. You gain the following benefits.
Choose a Sangromancy spell from any spell list of a level for which you have spell slots. You always have that spell prepared. You can cast it once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also cast the spell using any spell slot you have.
Sangromantic Initiate.entries.1.name: Blood Magic
You have a pool of two d12s that you can expend instead of {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB} when you cast Sangromancy spells. Your pool regains all expended dice when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Sangromantic Initiate.entries.2.name: Sanguine Potency
Sangromantic Initiate.name: Sangromantic Initiate
Savage Attacker.entries.0: You've trained to deal particularly damaging strikes. Once per turn when you hit a target with a weapon, you can roll the weapon's damage dice twice and use either roll against the target.
Savage Attacker.name: Savage Attacker
Scion of the Outer Planes.entries.0: Your connection to an Outer Plane infuses you with the energy there. Choose a type of plane listed in the Planar Infusion table. Your choice gives you resistance to a damage type and the ability to cast a cantrip, as specified in the table. You can cast this cantrip without material components, and your spellcasting ability for it is Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma (choose when you select this feat).
Scion of the Outer Planes.name: Scion of the Outer Planes
Second Chance.entries.0: Fortune favors you when someone tries to strike you. You gain the following benefits:
Second Chance.entries.1.items.0: When a creature you can see hits you with an attack roll, you can use your reaction to force that creature to reroll. Once you use this ability, you can't use it again until you roll initiative at the start of combat or until you finish a short or long rest.
Second Chance.name: Second Chance
Sentinel.entries.1.entries.0: Immediately after a creature within 5 feet of you takes the {@action Disengage|XPHB} action or hits a target other than you with an attack, you can make an {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB} against that creature.
Sentinel.entries.1.name: Guardian
Sentinel.entries.2.entries.0: When you hit a creature with an {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB}, the creature's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} becomes 0 for the rest of the current turn.
Sentinel.entries.2.name: Halt
Sentinel.name: Sentinel
Servo Crafting.entries.0: You are skilled in the creation of {@creature servo|psk|servos}—tiny constructs that function as personal assistants. You can cast the {@spell find familiar} spell as a ritual, creating a {@creature servo|psk} to serve as your familiar instead of an animal. A {@creature servo|psk}'s statistics appear in the "Artifact Creatures" section of this document. In every other way, a servo familiar functions as described in the {@spell find familiar} spell.
Servo Crafting.entries.1: You can communicate telepathically with your servo familiar and perceive through its senses as long as you are on the same plane of existence. You can speak through your servo in your own voice.
Servo Crafting.entries.2: Additionally, when you take the {@action Attack} action, you can forgo one of your own attacks to allow your servo familiar to make one attack of its own.
Servo Crafting.name: Servo Crafting
Shadow-Touched.entries.0: Your exposure to the Shadowfell's magic grants you the following benefits.
Shadow-Touched.entries.1.entries.0: Choose one {@filter level 1 spell from the Illusion or Necromancy|spells|level=1|school=I;N} school of magic. You always have that spell and the {@spell Invisibility|XPHB} spell prepared. You can cast each of these spells without expending a spell slot. Once you cast either spell in this way, you can't cast that spell in this way again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also cast these spells using spell slots you have of the appropriate level. The spells' spellcasting ability is the ability increased by this feat.
Shadow-Touched.entries.1.name: Теневая Магия
Shadow-Touched.name: Shadow-Touched
Shadowmoor Hexer.entries.1.entries.0: You always have the {@spell Hex|XPHB} spell prepared. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for this spell (choose when you select this feat). You can cast it once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in that way when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also cast the spell using any spell slots you have.
Shadowmoor Hexer.entries.1.name: Hex
Shadowmoor Hexer.entries.2.entries.0: When a creature that you've cursed with {@spell Hex|XPHB} hits you with an attack roll, the creature takes Psychic damage equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}. A creature takes this damage only once per turn.
Shadowmoor Hexer.entries.2.name: Curse Magic
Shadowmoor Hexer.name: Shadowmoor Hexer
Shadowsteel Adept.entries.0: You have learned to tap into the dangerous but powerful art of Shadowsteel casting. You gain the following benefits.
Shadowsteel Adept.entries.1.entries.0: You always have Shadowsteel curses prepared, and you can cast them with any spell slots you have.
Shadowsteel Adept.entries.1.name: Curse Caster
