Shadowsteel Adept.entries.2.entries.0: While holding your {@item Shadowsteel Focus|GrimHollowPG24}, you gain a +1 bonus to spell attack rolls and to the saving throw DCs of your spells.
Shadowsteel Adept.entries.2.name: Shadowsteel's Bite
Shadowsteel Adept.entries.3.entries.0: If your {@item Shadowsteel Focus|GrimHollowPG24} is also a weapon, that weapon has a +1 bonus to weapon attack rolls and damage rolls.
Shadowsteel Adept.entries.3.name: Shadowsteel Weapon
Shadowsteel Adept.name: Shadowsteel Adept
Shadowsteel Master.entries.0: Your mastery over Shadowsteel grows stronger. You gain the following benefits.
Shadowsteel Master.entries.1.entries.0: While holding your {@item Shadowsteel Focus|GrimHollowPG24}, when you cast a spell that targets one or more creatures and requires an attack roll or saving throw, you can choose one target you hit or who failed the saving throw to also take {@damage 1d4} Necrotic damage for each spell slot level expended to cast the spell.
Shadowsteel Master.entries.1.name: Necrotic Harmony
Shadowsteel Master.entries.2.entries.0: If your {@item Shadowsteel Focus|GrimHollowPG24} is also a weapon, that weapon has a +2 bonus to weapon attack rolls and damage rolls. Additionally, once on each of your turns, when you hit a creature with a weapon attack roll using your {@item Shadowsteel Focus|GrimHollowPG24}, you can cause the target to take an extra {@damage 2d8} Necrotic damage.
Shadowsteel Master.entries.2.name: Necrotic Weapon
Shadowsteel Master.name: Shadowsteel Master
Sharpshooter.entries.1.entries.0: Your ranged attacks with weapons ignore {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Half Cover} and {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Three-Quarters Cover}.
Sharpshooter.entries.1.name: Bypass Cover
Sharpshooter.entries.2.entries.0: Being within 5 feet of an enemy doesn't impose {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on your attack rolls with Ranged weapons.
Sharpshooter.entries.2.name: Firing in Melee
Sharpshooter.entries.3.entries.0: Attacking at long range doesn't impose {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on your attack rolls with Ranged weapons.
Sharpshooter.entries.3.name: Long Shots
Sharpshooter.name: Sharpshooter
Shield Master.entries.1.entries.0: If you attack a creature within 5 feet of you as part of the {@action Attack|XPHB} action and hit with a Melee weapon, you can immediately bash the target with your {@item Shield|XPHB} if it's equipped, forcing the target to make a Strength saving throw ({@dc 8} plus your Strength modifier and {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}). On a failed save, you either push the target 5 feet from you or cause it to have the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition (your choice). You can use this benefit only once on each of your turns.
Shield Master.entries.1.name: Shield Bash
Shield Master.entries.2.entries.0: If you're subjected to an effect that allows you to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to take no damage if you succeed on the saving throw and are holding a {@item Shield|XPHB}.
Shield Master.entries.2.name: Interpose Shield
Shield Master.name: Shield Master
Skill Expert.entries.1.entries.0: Вы получаете владение одним навыком на ваш выбор.
Skill Expert.entries.1.name: Skill Proficiency
Skill Expert.entries.2.entries.0: Choose one skill in which you have proficiency but lack {@variantrule Expertise|XPHB}. You gain {@variantrule Expertise|XPHB} with that skill.
Skill Expert.entries.2.name: Мастерство (Expertise)
Skill Expert.name: Skill Expert
Skilled.entries.0: You gain proficiency in any combination of three {@table Skill List; Skills|XPHB|skills} or {@filter tools|items|type=tool;artisan's tools;instrument;gaming set} of your choice.
Skilled.entries.1.entries.0: You can take this feat more than once.
Skilled.entries.1.name: Повторяемое
Skilled.name: Skilled
Skulker.entries.1.entries.0: You have {@sense Blindsight|XPHB} with a range of 10 feet.
Skulker.entries.1.name: Слепое зрение
Skulker.entries.2.entries.0: You exploit the distractions of battle, gaining {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on any Dexterity ({@skill Stealth|XPHB}) check you make as part of the {@action Hide|XPHB} action during combat.
Skulker.entries.2.name: Fog of War
Skulker.entries.3.entries.0: If you make an attack roll while hidden and the roll misses, making the attack roll doesn't reveal your location.
Skulker.entries.3.name: Sniper
Skulker.name: Skulker
Slasher.entries.1.entries.0: Once per turn when you hit a creature with an attack that deals Slashing damage, you can reduce the {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} of that creature by 10 feet until the start of your next turn.
Slasher.entries.1.name: Hamstring
Slasher.entries.2.entries.0: When you score a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} that deals Slashing damage to a creature, it has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on attack rolls until the start of your next turn.
Slasher.entries.2.name: Enhanced Critical
Slasher.name: Slasher
Soul of the Storm Giant.entries.0: You've manifested the tempest magic emblematic of storm giants, granting you the following benefits:
As a bonus action, you surround yourself with an aura of magical wind and lightning that extends 10 feet from you in every direction but not through {@quickref Cover||3||total cover}. The aura lasts until the start of your next turn or until you are incapacitated. While the aura is active, you have resistance to lightning and thunder damage. In addition, attack rolls against you have disadvantage, and whenever another creature starts its turn within the aura, you can force the creature to make a Strength saving throw (DC equals 8 + your proficiency bonus + the modifier of the ability increased by this feat). On a failed save, the creature's speed is halved until the start of its next turn. You can use this bonus action a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.
Soul of the Storm Giant.name: Soul of the Storm Giant
Speedy.entries.1.name: Speed Increase
Speedy.entries.2.entries.0: When you take the {@action Dash|XPHB} action on your turn, {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB} doesn't cost you extra movement for the rest of that turn.
Speedy.entries.2.name: Dash over Difficult Terrain
Speedy.entries.3.entries.0: {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks} have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} against you.
Speedy.entries.3.name: Agile Movement
Speedy.name: Speedy
Spell Sniper.entries.1.entries.0: Your attack rolls for spells ignore {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Half Cover} and {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Three-Quarters Cover}.
Spell Sniper.entries.1.name: Bypass Cover
Spell Sniper.entries.2.entries.0: Being within 5 feet of an enemy doesn't impose {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on your attack rolls with spells.
Spell Sniper.entries.2.name: Casting in Melee
Spell Sniper.entries.3.entries.0: When you cast a spell that has a range of at least 10 feet and requires you to make an attack roll, you can increase the spell's range by 60 feet.
Spell Sniper.entries.3.name: Increased Range
Spell Sniper.name: Spell Sniper
Spellfire Adept.entries.1.entries.0: Once per turn, when a spell you cast deals Radiant damage, you can expend up to two {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB}, roll them, and add the total rolled to one damage roll of the spell.
Spellfire Adept.entries.1.name: Fueled Spellfire
Spellfire Adept.entries.2.entries.0: When you make a damage roll that deals Radiant damage, it ignores {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Radiant damage.
Spellfire Adept.entries.2.name: Searing Spellfire
Spellfire Adept.name: Spellfire Adept
Spellfire Spark.entries.1.entries.0: Once per turn, when you take damage from a spell or magical effect, you reduce the total damage taken by {@dice 1d4}. You can't use this benefit if you have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition.
Spellfire Spark.entries.1.name: Magic Absorption
Spellfire Spark.entries.2.entries.0: You learn the {@spell Sacred Flame|XPHB} cantrip. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for this spell (choose when you select this feat). You can also cast this cantrip as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Spellfire Spark.entries.2.name: Spellfire Flame
Spellfire Spark.name: Spellfire Spark
Squat Nimbleness.entries.0: You are uncommonly nimble for your race. You gain the following benefits:
Squat Nimbleness.entries.1.items.0: Increase your walking speed by 5 feet.
Squat Nimbleness.entries.1.items.1: You gain proficiency in the {@skill Acrobatics} or {@skill Athletics} skill (your choice).
Squat Nimbleness.entries.1.items.2: You have advantage on any Strength ({@skill Athletics}) or Dexterity ({@skill Acrobatics}) check you make to escape from being {@condition grappled}.
Squat Nimbleness.name: Squat Nimbleness
Squire of Solamnia.entries.0: Your training in the ways of the Knights of Solamnia grants you these benefits:
Squire of Solamnia.entries.1.items.0.ent: Mounting or dismounting costs you only 5 feet of movement.
Squire of Solamnia.entries.1.items.1.ent: Once per turn, when you make a weapon attack roll against a creature, you can cause the attack roll to have advantage. If the attack hits, you roll a {@dice d8} and add the number rolled as a bonus to the attack's damage roll. You can use this benefit a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, but a use is expended only if the attack hits. You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.
Squire of Solamnia.entries.2.entries.0: Knights of Solamnia are only ever members of one of their organization's orders. In the course of their training, most knights begin as members of the Knights of the Crown and then move on to join other orders. Whether a character follows this path or another, they retain what they learned as a member of an order even if they join another. Characters can change what knightly order they're a part of, but they always have access to any Knight of Solamnia feats they've acquired.
Squire of Solamnia.entries.2.name: Membership in Knightly Orders
Squire of Solamnia.name: Squire of Solamnia
Street Justice.entries.1.entries.0: Your allies have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attack rolls against a creature {@condition Grappled|XPHB} by you.
Street Justice.entries.1.name: Headlock
Street Justice.entries.2.entries.0: When you use {@item Chain|XPHB}, {@item Manacles|XPHB}, or {@item Rope|XPHB} to bind a creature, add your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus} to the DC to escape or burst the {@item Chain|XPHB}, {@item Manacles|XPHB}, or {@item Rope|XPHB}.
Street Justice.entries.2.name: Sturdy Knot
Street Justice.entries.3.entries.0: A creature's {@variantrule Hostile [Attitude]|XPHB|Hostile} attitude doesn't impose {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on your Charisma ({@skill Intimidation|XPHB}) checks to influence that creature.
Street Justice.entries.3.name: Tough Talk
Street Justice.name: Street Justice
Strike of the Giants.entries.0: You have absorbed primeval magic that gives you an echo of the might of giants. When you take this feat, choose one of the benefits listed below. Once per turn, when you hit a target with a melee weapon attack or a ranged weapon attack using a thrown weapon, you can imbue the attack with an additional effect depending on the benefit you chose:
Strike of the Giants.entries.1.entries.0: The target takes an extra {@damage 1d4} thunder damage. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw, or you become invisible to it until the start of your next turn or until immediately after you make an attack roll or cast a spell.
Strike of the Giants.entries.1.name: Cloud Strike
Strike of the Giants.entries.2.entries.0: The target takes an extra {@damage 1d10} fire damage.
Strike of the Giants.entries.2.name: Fire Strike
Strike of the Giants.entries.3.entries.0: The target takes an extra {@damage 1d6} cold damage. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a Constitution saving throw, or its speed is reduced to 0 until the start of your next turn.
Strike of the Giants.entries.3.name: Frost Strike
Strike of the Giants.entries.4.entries.0: The target takes an extra {@damage 1d6} damage of the weapon's type. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a Strength saving throw or have the prone condition.
Strike of the Giants.entries.4.name: Hill Strike
Strike of the Giants.entries.5.entries.0: The target takes an extra {@damage 1d6} force damage. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be pushed 10 feet from you in a straight line.
Strike of the Giants.entries.5.name: Stone Strike
Strike of the Giants.entries.6.entries.0: The target takes an extra {@damage 1d6} lightning damage. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a Constitution saving throw, or it has disadvantage on attack rolls until the start of your next turn.
Strike of the Giants.entries.6.entries.1: The saving throw DC for these effects equals 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Strength or Constitution modifier.
Strike of the Giants.entries.6.entries.2: You can use this feat a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.
Strike of the Giants.entries.6.name: Storm Strike
Strike of the Giants.name: Strike of the Giants
Strixhaven Initiate.entries.0: You have studied some magical theory and have learned a few spells associated with Strixhaven University.
Strixhaven Initiate.entries.1: Choose one of Strixhaven's colleges: Lorehold, Prismari, Quandrix, Silverquill, or Witherbloom. You learn two cantrips and one 1st-level spell based on the college you choose, as specified in the Strixhaven Spells table.
Strixhaven Initiate.entries.2: You can cast the chosen 1st-level spell without a spell slot, and you must finish a long rest before you can cast it in this way again. You can also cast the spell using any spell slots you have.
Strixhaven Initiate.entries.3: Your spellcasting ability for this feat's spells is Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma (choose when you select this feat).
Strixhaven Initiate.entries.4.caption: Strixhaven Spells
Strixhaven Initiate.entries.4.rows.0.1: Choose two from {@spell light}, {@spell sacred flame}, and {@spell thaumaturgy}.
Strixhaven Initiate.entries.4.rows.1.1: Choose two from {@spell fire bolt}, {@spell prestidigitation}, and {@spell ray of frost}.
Strixhaven Initiate.entries.4.rows.2.1: Choose two from {@spell druidcraft}, {@spell guidance}, and {@spell mage hand}.
Strixhaven Initiate.entries.4.rows.2.2: Choose one 1st-level {@filter druid or wizard spell|spells|level=1|class=druid;wizard}.
Strixhaven Initiate.entries.4.rows.3.1: Choose two from {@spell sacred flame}, {@spell thaumaturgy}, and {@spell vicious mockery}.
Strixhaven Initiate.entries.4.rows.3.2: Choose one 1st-level {@filter bard or cleric spell|spells|level=1|class=bard;cleric}.
Strixhaven Initiate.entries.4.rows.4.1: Choose two from {@spell chill touch}, {@spell druidcraft}, and {@spell spare the dying}.
Strixhaven Initiate.entries.4.rows.4.2: Choose one 1st-level {@filter druid or wizard spell|spells|level=1|class=druid;wizard}.
Strixhaven Initiate.name: Strixhaven Initiate
Strixhaven Mascot.entries.0: You have learned how to summon a Strixhaven mascot to assist you, granting you these benefits:
Strixhaven Mascot.entries.1.items.0: You can cast the {@spell find familiar} spell as a ritual. Your familiar can take the form of the mascot associated with the college you chose for the Strixhaven Initiate feat: a {@creature spirit statue mascot|scc} (Lorehold), an {@creature art elemental mascot|scc} (Prismari), a {@creature fractal mascot|scc} (Quandrix), an {@creature inkling mascot|scc} (Silverquill), or a {@creature pest mascot|scc} (Witherbloom).
Strixhaven Mascot.entries.1.items.1: When you take the {@action Attack} action on your turn, you can forgo one attack to allow your mascot familiar to make one attack of its own with its reaction.
Strixhaven Mascot.entries.1.items.2: If your mascot familiar is within 60 feet of you, you can teleport as an action, swapping places with the familiar. If your destination space is too small for you to occupy, the teleportation fails and is wasted. Once you teleport in this way, you can't do so again until you finish a long rest, unless you expend a spell slot of 2nd level or higher to do it again.
Strixhaven Mascot.name: Strixhaven Mascot
Survivor.entries.0: Your ability to make the best out of a bad situation has served you well. You gain the following benefits.
Survivor.entries.1.entries.0: You need half the amount of food per day based on your size.
Survivor.entries.1.name: Hardy
Survivor.entries.2.entries.0: When you take the {@action Study|XPHB} action, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Intelligence checks made when taking the {@action Study|XPHB} action.
Survivor.entries.2.name: Intuitive
Survivor.entries.3.entries.0: Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}, your {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} level, if any, decreases by 1. Additionally, when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, your {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} level decreases by 2.
Survivor.entries.3.name: Shake It Off
Survivor.name: Survivor
Svirfneblin Magic.entries.0: You have inherited the innate spellcasting ability of your ancestors. This ability allows you to cast {@spell nondetection} on yourself at will, without needing a material component. You can also cast each of the following spells once with this ability: {@spell blindness/deafness}, {@spell blur}, and {@spell disguise self}. You regain the ability to cast these spells when you finish a long rest.
Svirfneblin Magic.entries.1: Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for these spells, and you cast them at their lowest possible levels.
Svirfneblin Magic.name: Svirfneblin Magic
Syndicate Spy.entries.0: Rising in the ranks of the Ebon Syndicate has taught you the clandestine skills of spycraft, valuable in your trade as a racketeer. You gain the following benefits.
Syndicate Spy.entries.1.entries.0: You have proficiency with {@item Thieves' Tools|XPHB}. While holding {@item Thieves' Tools|XPHB}, you can craft a key to a lock by spending 10 minutes with that lock.
Syndicate Spy.entries.1.name: Locksmith
Syndicate Spy.entries.2.entries.0: You have proficiency with a {@item Disguise Kit|XPHB}. If you spend 10 minutes donning a {@item Costume|XPHB},  you decide what you look like, including your height, weight, facial features, sound of your voice, hair length, coloration, and other distinguishing characteristics. You can make yourself appear as a member of another species, though none of your statistics change. You can't appear as a creature of a different size, and your basic shape stays the same; if you're bipedal, you can't use this benefit to become quadrupedal, for instance.
Syndicate Spy.entries.2.name: Master of Disguise
Syndicate Spy.entries.3.entries.0: You have proficiency with a {@item Forgery Kit|XPHB}. You aren't limited to 10 or fewer words when mimicking someone else's handwriting.
Syndicate Spy.entries.3.name: Master Calligrapher
Syndicate Spy.entries.4.entries.0: When using the {@action Hide|XPHB} action to conceal yourself from creatures that aren't {@variantrule Hostile [Attitude]|XPHB|Hostile} to you, you need only be {@variantrule Lightly Obscured|XPHB} and you may use Charisma instead of Dexterity for the check.
Syndicate Spy.entries.4.name: Blending In
Syndicate Spy.name: Syndicate Spy
Tavern Brawler.entries.1.entries.0: When you hit with your {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} and deal damage, you can deal Bludgeoning damage equal to {@damage 1d4} plus your Strength modifier instead of the normal damage of an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}.
Tavern Brawler.entries.1.name: Enhanced Unarmed Strike
Tavern Brawler.entries.2.entries.0: Whenever you roll a damage die for your {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, you can reroll the die if it rolls a 1, and you must use the new roll.
Tavern Brawler.entries.2.name: Damage Rerolls
Tavern Brawler.entries.3.entries.0: You have proficiency with improvised weapons.
Tavern Brawler.entries.3.name: Improvised Weaponry
Tavern Brawler.entries.4.entries.0: When you hit a creature with an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} as part of the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn, you can deal damage to the target and also push it 5 feet away from you. You can use this benefit only once per turn.
Tavern Brawler.entries.4.name: Толчок
Tavern Brawler.name: Tavern Brawler
Telekinetic.entries.1.entries.0: You learn the {@spell Mage Hand|XPHB} spell. You can cast it without Verbal or Somatic components, you can make the spectral hand {@condition Invisible|XPHB}, and its range and the distance it can be away from you both increase by 30 feet when you cast it. The spell's spellcasting ability is the ability increased by this feat.
Telekinetic.entries.1.name: Minor Telekinesis
Telekinetic.entries.2.entries.0: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can telekinetically shove one creature you can see within 30 feet of yourself. When you do so, the target must succeed on a Strength saving throw ({@dc 8} plus the ability modifier of the score increased by this feat and your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}) or be moved 5 feet toward or away from you.
Telekinetic.entries.2.name: Telekinetic Shove
Telekinetic.name: Telekinetic
Telepathic.entries.1.entries.0: You can speak telepathically to any creature you can see within 60 feet of yourself. Your telepathic utterances are in a language you know, and the creature understands you only if it knows that language. Your communication doesn't give the creature the ability to respond to you telepathically.
Telepathic.entries.1.name: Telepathic Utterance
Telepathic.entries.2.entries.0: You always have the {@spell Detect Thoughts|XPHB} spell prepared. You can cast it without a spell slot or spell components, and you must finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} before you can cast it in this way again. You can also cast it using spell slots you have of the appropriate level. Your spellcasting ability for the spell is the ability increased by this feat.
Telepathic.entries.2.name: Detect Thoughts
Telepathic.name: Telepathic
Thrown Weapon Fighting.entries.0: When you hit with a ranged attack roll using a weapon that has the {@itemProperty T|XPHB|Thrown} property, you gain a +2 bonus to the damage roll.
Thrown Weapon Fighting.name: Thrown Weapon Fighting
Thrown Weapon Master.entries.0: You excel at thrown weapons. You gain the following benefits.
Thrown Weapon Master.entries.1.entries.0: After you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action to make a ranged attack roll using a Simple weapon that has {@itemProperty T|XPHB|Thrown}, you can make two additional ranged attacks using Simple weapons that have {@itemProperty T|XPHB|Thrown} as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.
Thrown Weapon Master.entries.1.name: Multithrow
Thrown Weapon Master.entries.2.entries.0: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can retrieve and stow or wield all Simple weapons with {@itemProperty T|XPHB|Thrown} that are within 5 feet of you. You need a free hand to perform this maneuver.
Thrown Weapon Master.entries.2.name: Quick Hands
Thrown Weapon Master.entries.3.entries.0: Simple weapons with {@itemProperty T|XPHB|Thrown} that you are proficient with also have the Returning property (see Chapter 8: Advanced Weapons & Equipment).
Thrown Weapon Master.entries.3.name: Returning
Thrown Weapon Master.name: Thrown Weapon Master
Tireless Reveler.entries.0: When an ally you can see within 60 feet of yourself expends {@variantrule Heroic Inspiration|XPHB}, you can gain {@variantrule Heroic Inspiration|XPHB} if you lack it. You can use this benefit a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Tireless Reveler.name: Tireless Reveler
Tough.entries.0: Your {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximum increases by an amount equal to twice your character level when you gain this feat. Whenever you gain a character level thereafter, your {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximum increases by an additional 2 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}.
Tough.name: Tough
Treacherous Allure.entries.1.entries.0: You always have the {@spell Charm Person|XPHB} spell prepared. You can cast it without a spell slot, and you must finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} before you can cast it in this way again. You can also cast it using spell slots you have of the appropriate level. Your spellcasting ability for the spell is the ability increased by this feat.
Treacherous Allure.entries.1.name: Enchanting Presence
Treacherous Allure.entries.2.entries.0: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on attack rolls against creatures with the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} condition.
Treacherous Allure.entries.2.name: Inevitable Betrayal
Treacherous Allure.name: Treacherous Allure
Triage Expert.entries.0: You are trained in dealing with injuries on the battlefield, and with the saving of lives afterward. You gain the following benefits.
Triage Expert.entries.1.entries.0: Whenever you cause a creature to regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} or the Blood and Bone benefit, you can roll one extra die and discard the lowest die.
Triage Expert.entries.1.name: Bedside Manner
Triage Expert.entries.2.entries.0: Taking the {@action Utilize|XPHB} action and expending the use of a {@item Healer's Kit|XPHB} lets you heal a creature within 5 feet of you. The creature can expend and roll a {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB|Hit Point Die} and regain that number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}.
Triage Expert.entries.2.name: Blood and Bone
Triage Expert.name: Triage Expert
Two-Weapon Fighting.entries.0: When you make an extra attack as a result of using a weapon that has the {@itemProperty L|XPHB|Light} property, you can add your ability modifier to the damage of that attack if you aren't already adding it to the damage.
Two-Weapon Fighting.name: Бой двумя оружиями
Tyro of the Gauntlet.entries.1.entries.0: When an ally within 5 feet of you is subjected to an effect that would push or pull it, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to prevent that ally from being pushed or pulled. To receive this benefit, the ally can't have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition.
Tyro of the Gauntlet.entries.1.name: Stand as One
Tyro of the Gauntlet.entries.2.entries.0: When you take the {@action Ready|XPHB} action, the next attack roll made against you has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} before the start of your next turn.
Tyro of the Gauntlet.entries.2.name: Vigilant
Tyro of the Gauntlet.name: Tyro of the Gauntlet
Unarmed Fighting.entries.0: When you hit with your {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB} and deal damage, you can deal Bludgeoning damage equal to {@damage 1d6} plus your Strength modifier instead of the normal damage of an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}. If you aren't holding any weapons or a {@item Shield|XPHB} when you make the attack roll, the {@dice d6} becomes a {@dice d8}.
Unarmed Fighting.entries.1: At the start of each of your turns, you can deal {@damage 1d4} Bludgeoning damage to one creature {@condition Grappled|XPHB} by you.
Unarmed Fighting.name: Unarmed Fighting
Vampire Hunter.entries.1.entries.0: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on checks to escape from nonmagical restraints or the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition.
Vampire Hunter.entries.1.name: Adroit Escape
Vampire Hunter.entries.2.entries.0: When you take Necrotic damage, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to mitigate the damage. Roll a number of d6s equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and add them together. Reduce the Necrotic damage you take by this total. Once you use this benefit, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Vampire Hunter.entries.2.name: Vitality Ward
Vampire Hunter.name: Vampire Hunter
Vampire Touched.entries.1.entries.0: Choose one level 1 spell from the Enchantment or Illusion school of magic. You always have that spell and the {@spell Spider Climb|XPHB} spell prepared. You can cast each of these spells without expending a spell slot, but when you cast {@spell Spider Climb|XPHB} this way, you must target yourself, and you must finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} before you can cast each spell in this way again. You can also cast either spell using spell slots you have of the appropriate level. Your spellcasting ability for the spells is the ability increased by this feat.
Vampire Touched.entries.1.name: Vampire Magic
Vampire Touched.name: Vampire Touched
Vampires Plaything.entries.1.entries.0: When you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can create one {@item Potion of Healing|XDMG} or an {@item Antitoxin|XPHB}, as long as you have an empty {@item vial|XPHB} or {@item flask|XPHB}. These liquids evaporate when you finish another {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Vampires Plaything.entries.1.name: Decanting
Vampires Plaything.entries.2.entries.0: You can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to take the {@action Dash|XPHB} action or the {@action Disengage|XPHB} action. You can use this benefit a number of times equal to your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}, and you recover all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Vampires Plaything.entries.2.name: Timely Retreat
Vampires Plaything.entries.3.entries.0: The DM determines the fate of your former vampire master. While you and your former vampire master are on the same plane of existence, the vampire can communicate with you telepathically, and you can choose to allow the vampire to perceive through your senses.
Vampires Plaything.entries.3.name: Vampiric Connection
Vampires Plaything.name: Vampire's Plaything
Vampiric Exultation.entries.0: As an action, you can transform the lower half of your body into an inky black vapor, allowing you to float through the air. While transformed, you have a flying speed of 30 feet. You can maintain this form for up to 10 minutes. Once you use this ability, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.
Vampiric Exultation.name: Vampiric Exultation
Vigor of the Hill Giant.entries.0: You've manifested the resilience emblematic of hill giants, granting you the following benefits:
When you are subjected to an effect that would move you at least 5 feet or give you the prone condition, you can use your reaction to steady yourself. You aren't moved and don't have the prone condition.
Whenever you eat food as part of a short rest and spend one or more Hit Dice to regain hit points, you regain additional hit points equal to your Constitution modifier + your proficiency bonus.
Vigor of the Hill Giant.name: Vigor of the Hill Giant
War Caster.entries.1.entries.0: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Constitution saving throws that you make to maintain {@status Concentration|XPHB}.
War Caster.entries.1.name: Концентрация
War Caster.entries.2.entries.0: When a creature provokes an {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB} from you by leaving your reach, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to cast a spell at the creature rather than making an {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB}. The spell must have a casting time of one action and must target only that creature.
War Caster.entries.2.name: Reactive Spell
War Caster.entries.3.entries.0: You can perform the Somatic components of spells even when you have weapons or a {@item Shield|XPHB} in one or both hands.
War Caster.entries.3.name: Somatic Components
War Caster.name: War Caster
Weapon Master.entries.1.entries.0: Your training with weapons allows you to use the mastery property of one kind of Simple or Martial weapon of your choice, provided you have proficiency with it. Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can change the kind of weapon to another eligible kind.
Weapon Master.entries.1.name: Mastery Property
Weapon Master.name: Weapon Master
Witch Hunter.entries.0: You have honed and perfected your skills fighting against spellcasters, possibly from your background hunting mages as part of the Arcanist Inquisition. You gain the following benefits.
Witch Hunter.entries.1.entries.0: You can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to avoid a spell that is targeting only you and doesn't create an area of effect. Make a Wisdom saving throw against the spellcaster's spell save DC. On a successful save, the creature must choose a new target or the spell is canceled. A canceled spell dissipates with no effect, and any resources used to cast it are wasted.
Witch Hunter.entries.1.name: Dodge Spells
Witch Hunter.entries.2.entries.0: When you hit a target that can cast spells with an attack roll using a Melee weapon or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}, you can reduce its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} by 15 feet until the start of your next turn.
Witch Hunter.entries.2.name: Keep Your Enemies Close
Witch Hunter.entries.3.entries.0: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against all Shadowsteel curses, and also against spells with a duration of more than 10 minutes.
Witch Hunter.entries.3.name: Resist Curses
Witch Hunter.name: Witch Hunter
Wood Elf Magic.entries.0: You learn the magic of the primeval woods, which are revered and protected by your people. You learn one druid cantrip of your choice. You also learn the {@spell longstrider} and {@spell pass without trace} spells, each of which you can cast once without expending a spell slot. You regain the ability to cast these two spells in this way when you finish a long rest. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for all three spells.
Wood Elf Magic.name: Wood Elf Magic
Zhentarim Ruffian.entries.1.entries.0: When you roll damage for an {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB}, you can roll the damage dice twice and use either roll against the target.
Zhentarim Ruffian.entries.1.name: Exploit Opening
Zhentarim Ruffian.entries.2.entries.0: If you have {@variantrule Heroic Inspiration|XPHB} when you roll {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB}, you can expend it to give yourself and your allies {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on that {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} roll.
Zhentarim Ruffian.entries.2.name: Family First
Zhentarim Ruffian.name: Zhentarim Ruffian
Zhentarim Tactics.entries.1.entries.0: Immediately after a creature within 5 feet of you hits you with a melee attack, you can make an {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB} against that creature.
Zhentarim Tactics.entries.1.name: Retaliate
Zhentarim Tactics.entries.2.entries.0: When you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, choose a skill in which you have proficiency. You have {@variantrule Expertise|XPHB} in that skill until you finish your next {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
Zhentarim Tactics.entries.2.name: Versatile Merc
Zhentarim Tactics.name: Zhentarim Tactics
